## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
(empty — flag in metadata)

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
Enhancements to the Units card and all instances where Units card data is driving important functions.
Better management of Dual Units
Allowing either pack or serving first



Easier UX flows for pack-serving linking, editing individual records, etc.
always able to add sub-qty for eligible items, regardless of whether or not a Weight value is present



Easier actions for Inventory readiness options (Base Unit settings)

Identifier enhancements
Identifier icons as action buttons

Identifier  legend

UI display improvements (scrolling chip?)



Moving ingredient yield factor to the Units card to be per serving

================ NXT-36067 ================
NXT-36067 | [Done Done] [Epic] [P:Highest] IM - Item Units & Sub-types
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: -  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
(empty — flag in metadata)

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
This epic is part of a multi-epic effort to rework Item Management in 2023 to consolidate the various item types and eliminate redundancy in shared use cases.
Epic Timeline
  

 

  (this epic)

 

 



Revised IM Structure (Use case visualization; not backend)
Expanded Hierarchy Concept


Notes on Combined Servings and Pack section
Combine Pack Size and Serving Measures section into a single cohesive section so that the pack-to-serving conversion is not entirely dependent on weight.
Goals:
Combine the sections

Preserve the ability to “skip” pack sizes for POS info
We will create a special “POS only” screen for these types of “items”



Enhance the use of FMV to allow independent price entry
Getting more flexible with costing, potentially allowing entry on serving sizes instead of only highest pack size. 
Make it optional as well. Leverage the concept of the item type statuses (incomplete for costing, etc.)





Leverage the combined section to allow ‘Sub Quantity’ to be used for linking Pack Units to Serving Measures, instead of Weight
Also allow serving sizes to be pack sizes as well, for base units only



Add a flag for Fluid Item which would apply to the entire item and entire section
This would trigger the use of the Volume instead of weight

Only exception would be for Pack Units which would be physical weight in most cases



Change Serving Size Description to text field instead of master dropdown
Accommodating GDSN data structure with text string instead of split fields

0.64 OZ SERVING, Varied  Servings Per Container




Obstacles:
Location on page UI 
This section applies to many tabs and many variations of the item; it’s difficult to place in a single location

This section is always a prerequisite for most other sections (at least to an extent) so it must take place before the tabs



Inferring flags vs. user entry
We need to identify the Base Unit so we can effectively know when the Pack Size section stops

We likely don’t want to ask users to explicitly differentiate between Pack units and Serving units, so we should try to infer
maybe by using the Serving Size Qty field

we will not infer





Indicating flags/attributes without using dedicated columns
We want to show the ways in which particular units/measures are being used (ex: for the Menu Item, for the Prep Site, etc.) but need to avoid having dedicated columns for each of these things

Potentially use icons (w/ tooltips) or something similar



Differentiating between various unit/serving types by using UI features
For example, even though it’s a combined section we still need to differentiate between units (what shows up in Inventory), servings (for recipes, etc.), and any other use case

This may rely somewhat heavily on icons, colors, styling, etc.




General Rules:
Sub-Qty is for pack sizes, except the one case where serving size is linked to the base unit

Global Info (Brand, Manufacturer, Product Code) is always optional, but ideally available

Inventory flag is trig

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Menu Planning Manager, I would like to be able to have an area where I can take care of my menu item management, add menu items, do menu item category configurations to handle anything related to menu items.
This epic is part of a multi-epic effort to rework Item Management in 2023 to consolidate the various item types and eliminate redundancy in shared use cases.
Epic Timeline
  

 

 

 (this epic)

 


Menu Item Overhaul
Required to provide POS Pricing for Recipe-based Menu Items

Prerequisite for some later features such as Centrally Produced Items



Required enhancements for Components, Variety Menu Items, Nutrient rules, etc.

Menu Item History
Detail Card

Recipe Details 

Nutrient Details

Contributions

Variety Menu Item changes



6.3+ Enhancements
Deconstructed Menu Item (required for full Prep Site functionality)

Non-Food Menu Items




Menu + POS Entry Points
Menu Item first
Menu Item (Custom) 
not linked to anything

not directly linked 1-to-1; linked to multiple recipes



Variety Menu Item

Reverse direct linked (new; needed) “Create from….”
to a single recipe

to a single item



Menu Item w/ POS



POS Only 

Generic POS Item (future)

Recipe into Menu Item (existing)
Recipe on POS



Item into Menu Item (existing; “DMI”)
Item on POS




Flags/Identifiers
POS only

================ NXT-37441 ================
NXT-37441 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Highest] IM - Items - Units - Link Servings to Pack Size
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide Sub-Qty on the Serving Size row in valid scenarios 

Emphasize Weight field for Pack Size base units when Sub-Qty is not used for linking

Trigger Inventory readiness based on proper pack-serving links

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a user configuring items I want the requirement to link servings and packs when both exist so that my inventory item is always linked to the MP/PD version of the item.
Requirements
Continuing from  

Emphasize Sub-Qty or Weight as required to ensure Pack Sizes and Servings are linked
Sub-Qty 
if the Serving Size is potentially inventory-able (Amount = 1), enable the Sub-Qty for the Serving Size row

“Please add another unit, enter sub-quantity, or enter pack size weight”
Sub-Qty: “How many [description of serving] are in [description of pack unit]?”



Weight 

For Pack Size units, Weight is optional, but may be used to link to a weight-based serving instead of Sub-Qty

“Please add another unit, enter sub-quantity, or enter pack size weight”
Weight: “Enter weight for the  [description of pack unit]



If a serving size is already added

if this is a second or third pack size being added, but it’s being added as a “lower” unit (again, assuming this may be the base unit, we’ll need weight) below the existing pack size unit(s)

If it’s the lowest unit in a 3-tier pack size
If they indicated the item as Food and should be adding servings later, we should emphasize obtaining weight on the lowest unit







Require linking for Inventory readiness
We will allow users to add pack units which are unlinked from servings, but these items will be Inventory Ineligible (Inventory Readiness)
If servings and pack units are not linked, this prompt will replace the Confirm Base Unit step

================ NXT-37110 ================
NXT-37110 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Pack Sizes & Sub-Qty
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide the ability to add pack size units in the combined Units grid

Bring attention to the appropriate Sub-Qty fields as required to link Pack Sizes

Emphasize Weight field for rare scenarios where required

Build concept of Inventory Readiness for item status and create item flow logic

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a user configuring items I want the ability to add pack size units in the Units section so I can begin building the pack size for my Inventory item.
Requirements
Provide the ability to add a pack size unit in the new Units grid

Display an ‘+ Pack’ button which would insert a new row in the Units grid

Capture only the initial mandatory fields:


Description
this should be a hybrid smart text field (NXT-37052)

we should match pack size units in the same way we will match serving description

recommend common measures configured (Case, Bag, etc.)
consider adding a setting to restrict users to only the configured options

Require at least one character before showing suggestions





Sub-Qty - only in some scenarios
Sub-Qty on the same row
if the current pack size being added is a second pack size, being added as a child item to (below) the existing pack size



Sub-Qty on the child item row
if the current pack size being added is a second pack size, being added as a parent to (above) the existing pack size



Do not enable Sub-Qty for the first pack size being added

Prototype Flows : 
2.a  
2.b  
2.c  



Weight - optional, except for linking to servings
 

“Enter weight (optional) if this unit will be the base unit”




Keep optional/other fields non-editable
Amount - this will never apply to pack size units; only used for Serving Size

Cost / Value - enabled at the end during finish tasks
User can select the specific pack size (or inventory-able serving) unit on which to enter the pricing






Inventory Readiness  
General Rules:
Sub-Qty is for pack sizes, except the one case where serving size is linked to the base unit

Inventory flag is triggered if Pack Sizes are added

at least one serving size or pack size unit should be configured before moving on

================ NXT-35898 ================
NXT-35898 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Card & Add Serving
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide the new Units card in the item creation header which is the combination of the pack size and serving size sections

Provide Food item and Fluid item questions in the header bar of the card before enabling the grid

After questions, load the grid with Add Serving Size and Add Pack Size actions (based on question input)

Display a header bar area for various prompts or questions provided to the user 

Provide a button for adding a Serving Size unit

Provide the ability to add and save independent serving sizes

Provide a button for adding Pack Size units

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
Combine Pack Size and Serving Measures section into a single cohesive section so that the pack-to-serving conversion is not entirely dependent on weight.
This is the main story for building the new combined section to manage all pack size units and serving sizes in one place, while still managing the unique contexts of these separate units.
*Note: this card will have many view-mode features and will also manage all the business rules for editing, but these initial stories are in the context of the new create flow. We will revise as needed the further we get into the item flow.
Requirements
Display a ‘Units’ card in the header of Items next to the general info card
This card should be editable once the general card is saved



Provide initial questions in the header bar of the card before enabling the grid
Food Item? Y/N
if Yes, ask Fluid Item

if No, disable Serving Sizes



Fluid Item? Y/N
if Yes, switch to ‘Volume’ for Serving Sizes





After questions, load the grid with Add Serving Size and Add Pack Size actions (based on question input)

Display a header bar area for various prompts or questions provided to the user  

Provide a button for adding a Serving Size unit -  
Allow the user to add independent serving sizes

Amount field
if = 1, eligible for having sub-quantity and for possibly becoming a pack size (base unit)
“Potentially inventory-able serving”



if anything other than 1, it’s a serving size only (current behavior)



Description
this should be a hybrid smart text field instead of a dropdown (NXT-37052)

we should still have the standard measures and try to match them in the text field (hence the ‘smart’ text field)



Weight / Volume
Column header label rules
Use ‘Weight’ if the item is not fluid

Use ‘Weight / Volume’ if the item is fluid (NXT-37054)
this is because we will use fluid measures for the servings but weight for (most of the) pack sizes





Provide the separate number and measure dropdown control





Provide a button for adding Pack Size units -  

Display the following columns for the grid
Amount - a field only applicable to Serving Sizes

Description - a smart text field used for both serving and pack size units 
this will reference the new Common Measures page



Sub-Qty - a field used to link pack size units, and in some rare cases, used to link a serving size to a pack size base unit

Weight / Volume - field used to define the measurable unit of the item 
Use ‘Weight’ header label if the item is not fluid
this is the current ‘Weight in Grams’ column



Use ‘Weight / Volume’ header label if the item is fluid, because servings will be volume but packs will be weight
this Volume column essentially replaces ‘Weight in Grams’

this can be thought of like ‘Volume in Milliliter' 
and just like the Grams column has Ounce and Pound, the Volume column will also have all the scalable volume units (Quart, Tbsp, etc.)







Cost / Value - the replacement field for Fair Market Value
this will now be editable on all elig

================ NXT-33518 ================
NXT-33518 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Low] IM/INV - Items - Units - Option for counting Base Unit in decimals
Module: Inventory  |  components: Inventory  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide an option in the context of the new Units card to determine whether the Base Unit can be counted in decimals

Update Inventory to consume the decimal flag to enable or disable the presence of decimals throughout the module for that item

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a District Admin I want the ability to decide whether an item’s base unit should be able to be counted in decimals in Inventory so I can ensure we are counting items according to their real pack size details.
Example: a milk carton will never be counted in decimals, but a bag of flour would be.
Requirements
Provide an option in the context of the new Units card to determine whether the Base Unit can be counted in decimals
TBD when this option is locked 



If base unit does not allow decimals, the item will always be restricted to being counted in whole numbers

Update Inventory to consume the decimal flag to enable or disable the presence of decimals throughout the module for that item
Separate future story





Mockups :

================ NXT-37568 ================
NXT-37568 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Items - Units - Save & Continue - Costing
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide questions/prompts to complete the overall required fields for saving the initial Units card and proceeding to item creation

Provide an optional Costing step

Require FMV for Inventory readiness

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a user configuring items I want a series of completion tasks in the new Units card so I can configure any serving or pack size data points that aren’t input on a specific row or add flow.
Requirements
Provide questions/prompts to complete the overall required fields for saving the initial Units card and proceeding to item creation
this will have a lot of overlap with the Text Guidance story



Execute required checks before enabling official Save
1. Confirm Inventory readiness / Base Unit -  

2. Prompt for Costing (this story)

These checks will always apply when saving the card, but these are presented as “steps” when creating the first time

Once saved, these 2 checks will be handled differently in edit/update scenarios.
The checks will still occur, but will be managed by icons/banners





Provide a Costing prompt - “Please add costing for the most relevant unit”
Enable the Cost/Value field to be edited for all Pack Size units or a linked Serving Size (linked via Sub-Qty)
if only Serving Sizes exist, allow user to enter a cost for any serving sizes with '1' as the Amount



Provide the ability to Skip 
Flag the item as ‘Missing Costing’ if the user skips the costing step (this will be indicated on the card header in view mode)





Consider Inventory Readiness for Costing validations
if the Valuation Group selected has a ‘Standard Price’ for Valuation Method, Costing is required for Inventory
this is because FMV is used for the base valuation within Inventory

other Valuation Methods do not reference FMV



Require FMV for Inventory readiness
we may “loosen” this in the future so items can be brought into Inventory without FMV







General Rules:
Sub-Qty is for pack sizes, except the one case where serving size is linked to the base unit

Global Info (Brand, Manufacturer, Product Code) is always optional, but ideally available

Inventory flag is triggered if Pack Sizes are added

Item Categorizations are only required if Inventory is enabled 

at least one serving size should be configured before moving on


Mockups :

================ NXT-37109 ================
NXT-37109 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Items - Units - Unit Identifiers
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Populate the Identifiers column with various icons associated with different important flags which are assigned at a unit level

Indicate typical Serving Size identifiers such as Nutrient association, Contribution association, etc.

Add new identifiers to better help explain the expanded concepts of the Units card

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As an Item Management user I want a column which explains the different use cases of my various units so I can better understand the complex consumption of the serving and pack size units.
Unit-level Identifiers
Base Unit
only if the base serving is also a pack size 



Serving Size identifiers (existing)

Cluster within existing singular icon
Nutrition assigned 

Contributions assigned

Used on a recipe



Preferred Measure
Show a star/favorite icon for preferred measure

this can be clicked in edit mode to indicate a preferred serving size



Serving Size (new)
Used for Menu Item (MI)

Used for Single Item Recipe (SIR)



Possible others
$ icon on the unit which had the manual cost entry
since costs are scaled for all units in view mode, we want to show the unit for which the price was actually entered by the user







Mockups :

================ NXT-37111 ================
NXT-37111 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Save & Continue - Inventory Ready
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide questions/prompts to complete the overall required fields for saving the initial Units card and proceeding to item creation

Provide a step to confirm Inventory info, including the Base Unit

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a user configuring items I want a series of completion tasks in the new Units card so I can configure any serving or pack size data points that aren’t input on a specific row or add flow.
Requirements
Provide questions/prompts to complete the overall required fields for saving the initial Units card and proceeding to item creation

Execute required checks before enabling official Save
1. Confirm Inventory readiness / Base Unit (this story)

2. Prompt for Costing -  

These checks will always apply when saving the card, but these are presented as “steps” when creating the first time

Once saved, these 2 checks will be handled differently in edit/update scenarios.
The checks will still occur, but will be managed by icons/banners





Confirm Inventory Readiness / Base Unit - few scenarios
If pack sizes are incomplete/invalid (not linked, not added, etc.), we can use this step to confirm that the user is not configuring pack sizes
Ex: “This item is not inventory eligible with the current pack size configuration. Continue anyway?”
“To make pack sizes valid, link the pack and servings using weight or sub-quantity”





If there are fewer than 3 pack sizes and no inventory-able serving sizes linked (using sub-qty)
“Is [description of lowest unit] the smallest inventory-able unit?” Yes/No
Yes > Confirms base unit and proceeds

No > Emphasize the ‘Add Pack’ button 





If there is an inventory-able serving (linked via sub-qty) which is linked to the current base unit via sub-qty 
*Note: there must still be fewer than 3 pack sizes

“Is [description of lowest unit] or [description of linked serving size] the smallest inventory-able unit?
if lowest unit > confirms base unit and proceeds

if serving size > convert the serving size into a pack size (it’s both) and define it as the Base Unit



*Exception: this step/option is invalid once the item is in Inventory tables and Pack Size is locked



if there are no pack size units but there is at least one inventory-able serving 
 







General Rules:
Sub-Qty is for pack sizes, except the one case where serving size is linked to the base unit

Global Info (Brand, Manufacturer, Product Code) is always optional, but ideally available

Inventory flag is triggered if Pack Sizes are added

Item Categorizations are only required if Inventory is enabled 

at least one unit should be configured before moving on



Mockups :

================ NXT-40778 ================
NXT-40778 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Merge Unit logic & prompt
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-45562 — IM - Units Card Overhaul  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Prompt users with a dedicated ‘Merge’ button when they are attempting to create a unit which is likely intended to be a dual unit
this will be our primary method to capture dual units

this will also be how we allow single unit dual units



Implement a hard stop warning for matching Description of Weight/Volume
if Pack and Serving descriptions are the exact same

if Weight/Volume are the exact same

do this in placeholder mode as well



Implement a soft stop warning if Weight/Volume is within 15% of each other
just a warning, with the Merge prompt 

this logic is not executed until AFTER placeholder mode is completed



 Provide a ‘Merge w/ Serving’ prompt button in the Identifier column
when entering a duplicate description on a pack size



Once the Merge button is clicked, show a special editable mode in which the user can confirm the merging of the serving and pack
if the serving being promoted does not have sub-qty, then keep the user in edit mode and require input of sub-qty

if the serving has sub-qty, promote that sub-qty to the new base unit

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As an IM user I want the ability to make a pack unit into a serving size as well, so I can manage the “dual unit” scenario whether I create packs or servings first.

================ NXT-54229 ================
NXT-54229 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Rules for Editability
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-45562 — IM - Units Card Overhaul  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Certain functions should be enabled based on sub-type logic, rather than selected tab
Menu Serving and Exceptions should be available once the item is a Menu Item

Cost/Value should be available as soon as there is at least one unit

 GTIN/UPC should be available once there is a pack size unit or an inventory-able serving (1 Each)

Disable or gray out the Add button and Update button options when they’re not available
do not remove them from the button options





When to exit placeholder mode

Long-term editing:
changing weight for a recipe ingredient

changing weight for a DMI

changing description for an ingredient or DMI

changing pack size descriptions

changing sub-quantities
including inventory-able servings and dual units





Match banners/font/styling for Summary Info card

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a district admin or menu planner I want the Units card to behave the same across all tabs so I don’t have to memorize the UI logic to make important updates.

Summary Info card banner

Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -

Update the logic and UX behavior for Inventory Readiness

Remove the sequential logic of the inventory requirements
instead it should operate more like a status



Always display the inventory readiness banner
including for placeholder units, when the “# of countable inventory units” selection is 1, 2 or 3

there will be a status/banner for every scenario, including one for ‘Non-Inventory’ for Ineligible or Disabled

================ NXT-37068 ================
NXT-37068 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Units - Inventory Readiness
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-36067 — IM - Item Units & Sub-types  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Create the concept of Inventory Readiness or inventory publishing

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As an Item Management user I want the concept of Inventory Readiness so I can track which items are available to the Inventory module as well as manually control it.
Requirements
Create the concept of Inventory Readiness or inventory publishing
this will be a more explicit control of which items from IM can become available within the Inventory module
right now this is determined by whether or not the Product Info card is populated



Use Pack Size information to drive the concept of Inventory Readiness

Inventory Readiness will be displayed as a type of header level status on the Units card




View Mode 
Maintain the following statuses for Inventory readiness
Do Not Publish - manual control
User may have some information, but they are holding the item from being transferrable to Inventory yet

UI element TBD  
Alert icon becomes checkbox once valid pack size data is added





Inventory Ineligible - pack sizes are incomplete
Pack sizes missing / not added

Pack sizes are added but not linked to one another (preventing through UI)

Pack sizes exist but are not properly linked to servings



Inventory Incomplete - missing categories, but pack sizes are configured 

Tiers - when pack sizes are completed, show how many tiers are configured
this implies ‘Inventory Complete/Ready'





Display status in the Units header using an icon or symbol

Display tooltips for the various statuses explaining what data is required
Do Not Publish (hold from Inventory)
“This item is not Inventory Ready, per manual user control”



Inventory Ineligible
“Pack size units must be configured for Inventory eligibility”

“Pack size units must be linked to servings for Inventory eligibility”



Inventory Incomplete
“Item categorizations must be provided for Inventory readiness”



Inventory Complete*
*Note: this status is not explicitly displayed. Instead, display the number of pack size tiers 
Ex:  



Tooltip: “This 2-tier item is Inventory ready”

Tooltip if Pack Size locked: “This 3-tier item is published to Inventory”





Leverage the status/tooltips to draw focus to the appropriate corrective actions 
This is more appropriate for the Edit/Update story 




Mockups:
Manual Control

================ NXT-37815 ================
NXT-37815 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Items - Inventory Info Units card
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-37500 — IM - Consolidation Enhancement & Cleanup  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Requirements
Provide an Edit mode which is specific to the Inventory Info tab
this will have some overlap with the Nutrient Info edit (Pack sizes, Pricing)

filter by only pack size units and any servings linked via sub-qty or with ‘1' as amount and non-standard measure



Add an edit GTIN/UPC function to the Units card in edit mode
For Edit GTIN, adjust the edit mode of Units card and replace Identifiers column with a GTIN/UPC column and a text field for each unit



Allow 8-14 digits in the GTIN field
UPC: 12 digits

GTIN: 14 digits

anything other than 12 or 14 digits will not be a valid match and can be considered invalid entry

show a small alert icon for invalid data



Display the Pricing button in edit mode

Display barcode identifier for units which have GTIN

Provide informational guided messages in the banner bar

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Food Service Director I want an Inventory Info tab on the Item page so I can view and manage all inventory or product related details of the item such as GTIN/UPC numbers, item substitutions and expanded pack size details.

================ NXT-41273 ================
NXT-41273 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Items/Recipes/Menu Items - Images & Documents
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: External/Internal
Epic: NXT-40487 — IM - Production Usability Enhancements (2023)  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)
Release Notes Title: Item Management Images & Documents

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Replace the edit mode of the Images card with a new Image & Document slideout using the common component

In the slideout, use the image and file preview component

Provide a ‘Image & Document Options’ card below with Item Management specific use cases

For images, provide a ‘Use As’ dropdown for the preferred image types
Preferred Ingredient Image

Preferred Inventory Image

Menu Image (for publishing)



For documents, provide a ‘Use As’ dropdown for the document file types
CN Label

Buy American Exemption Letter

Product Formulation Statements 

Meal Credits

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
All item types within the Item Management module have been updated with a new Images & Documents feature, which replaces the previous Images-only card. Images & Documents is the top right card on each details page, and clicking the expand icon will display a drawer panel with a file upload tool. Up to 10 files can be uploaded with any combination of supported file types. Once images and documents are uploaded, they can be assigned with 'Use As' options in the table section below the upload tool. For image files, these Use As options include Preferred Image options for each Ingredient, Inventory, and Menu uses. These Preferred Image flags will determine which image is selected as the primary image when using that item in various sub-type contexts, such as displaying the Preferred Inventory Image when using the Ordering function. For both image and document files, there are document labels that can be assigned for tracking purposes, such as CN Label, Product Formulation Statement, or Buy American Exemption Letter.

## Release Notes (Internal)  [TIER 2]
The ‘Preferred Ingredient’ image will be prioritized on the Items page split view and when searching ingredients on recipes.
The ‘Preferred Inventory’ image will be prioritized on the Items page for non-food items and is shown on the Inventory Ordering cart.
The ‘Preferred Menu’ image will be displayed when searching and managing menu items on a Variety Menu Item, as well as when adding menu items to a Menu.

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As an Item Management user I want the ability to attach documents to items, recipes and other IM types so I may save things like product formulation statements, CN labels, crediting documents, and other important pieces of information.
Component Library - File Dropzone

================ NXT-43239 ================
NXT-43239 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items/Recipes/Menu Items - Nutrient Details updates
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: External/Internal
Epic: NXT-40487 — IM - Production Usability Enhancements (2023)  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)
Release Notes Title: Nutrient Details Improvements

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Redesign Nutrient Details to more effectively use the card space
in edit mode, rows should be less vertically stretched out, card should be shorter

Size the input fields to eliminate unnecessary width (i.e. we know the max length of values)

Change card to 50% page width
consider expanding to 2/3 when editing (optional)



Improve error handling
do not repeat red messages for each row

simply highlight the missing fields and provide a singular message





Automatically set nested nutrients to zero if the parent nutrient is set to 0
e.g. if ‘Total Fat’ is 0 then both Sat and Trans Fat will be 0

also for Carbohydrates and the nested Fiber/Sugar rows



Update business rules:
Vitamin A, Vitamin C, Moisture, Ash should be changed to optional

Moisture and Ash do not require a “Missing” checkbox as they are never present on nutrition labels



Consider 0% as valid input
Currently, vitamin % is not saving zeroes

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
The Nutrient Details card has been updated on all types within Item Management. The card has been redesigned to better utilize available space, and some functional enhancements have been made. Vitamin A and Vitamin C are now considered optional for nutrient entry, and leaving them blank will automatically enable the Is Missing option. Similarly, Moisture and Ash have been made optional and the Is Missing option has been removed, as these nutrients are generally not provided on labels. Data entry and error handling have also received some usability improvements.

## Release Notes (Internal)  [TIER 2]
Nutrient Details update also includes a resolution to an issue with entering 0 on Daily Value % not being accepted. Some data entry enhancements have been made for Total Fat and Total Carbohydrates; entering 0 for these values will now automatically populate the nested nutrients with 0 as well.

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Nutrition Manager I want the Nutrient Details card to be easier to use and read so I can treat it like a nutrition label.
Optimize sizing in most condensed views (split with nav expanded, etc.)
Nutrient entry not considering 0 when entered on DV column of Vitamins

================ NXT-24128 ================
NXT-24128 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Custom Allergen Configuration
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-13780 — IM - UI Improvements   (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Acceptance Criteria
This story is done when:
Entry Point
A Custom allergens option is added under IM configuration area

Option Custom Allergen


Result Grid
Should display fields as per mockup 


User should be able to search and sort fields on result grid
User should be able to edit Custom Allergen Description [Description Only]
Only for user created Custom Allergen (e.g CN cannot be edited or Deactivated)



User should be able to deactivate Custom Allergen only if there are no items/recipes/menus using it
User should be presented with a confirmation message “Are you sure you want to deactivate this Custom Allergen” is Yes proceed with deletion if no cancel process




When Slide Out Card is displayed
Fields on slide out card:
Custom Allergen Description [Free text box]
Is a Mandatory field

Only can be added to user/district created data source (e.g. Local)



Data Source [Dropdown box that list available data sources to the user] [Local]
Is a Mandatory field

Only one Data Source can be selected [Local]





When pressing the SAVE button
Validation occurs
Custom Allergen is Mandatory and no duplicates are allowed

If Custom Allergen already exist warn the user with a message “Custom Allergen already in use, please use a different Custom Allergen name and allow the user to enter a different name”

One Data Source is Selected (Mandatory)

The newly added Custom Allergen is listed on the result grid

System returns to Custom Allergen screen





If User clicks on the X icon then the Slide Out Card is close and transactions are canceled

Pagination should be present

Export [PDF Format]
Title [Custom Allergen Configuration]

Should cover same fields as the result grid [Custom Allergen Description, Data Source Name]

Header
Updated by [User Name]

Generated on {System date/time UTC]



Footer
Power by SchoolCafe for {School District Name]

Pagination should be present






General:
Once user selects configuration a pop-up window is presented to the user. This pop-up windows asks for data source and configuration option desired. When user click/press apply button then system re-directs the user to appropriate configuration area
Only use Local data source

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As an authorized Nutrition service user I need a way to create custom allergen  in order to help identify items with allergens

================ NXT-40447 ================
NXT-40447 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:Medium] IM - Menu Items - Servings - Menu Servings (scaled)
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-29833 — IM - Menu & POS Consolidation   (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Provide the ability to add additional serving sizes for the Menu Item which are scaled from the primary measure (or using weight/volume)

Separate this “Add Serving” function from the primary Add Serving function, as this function is only adding Menu Item servings

Within the Menu Item tab specific version of the Units card, provide an Edit mode

Within the MI Units edit mode, provide a “+Menu Serving” button

When +Menu Serving is clicked, insert a new serving size row into the grid
only Amount and Description are editable

pre-load Description with original Menu Item serving measure

Make Description a dropdown (not free text) with only the menu item measure and the corresponding weight or volume measures (also inherited from main measure)

Calculate Weight/Volume by scaling from original measure

Use row level Save icon to return to primary edit mode



Provide guided informational messages in the banner bar

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Menu Planner I want the ability to create additional scaled measures for menu item serving sizes so I can have all the different sizes I serve to students.

================ NXT-36583 ================
NXT-36583 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Recipe Rework - Recipe Steps - HACCP Process (Step 0)
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-26823 — IM - Item Type Consolidation - Recipe Rework  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Add a small selection for Recipe HACCP Process 
this will belong to the Steps process and tab

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Nutritionist I want the HACCP options available on recipes so I can easily assign required control points for my recipe preparation.
Requirements
Provide a small section on the recipe Steps & Ingredients tab above (or on, inside, next to, etc.) the Steps card
For now, this could be just “HACCP Process” but may be expanded on later
This may eventually be more accurately called “Pre-Steps” or something, and could contain other fields like Pre-Preparation Instructions



In the ‘Edit Recipe Steps' mode, enable the HACCP Process selection 
This will reflect the configured processes on the config page, but will just be No Cook, Same Day Service and Complex Food 98% of the time

This is the only required selection for this section



Provide independent Save/Cancel



Validate Saves against the added control points
Allow independent editing

Allow Process to be changed
If the user has CCPs added to the Steps which are not associated with the Process selected, warn the user
“This will clear some of the existing Control Points. Are you sure to change the HACCP Process?”



If the user switches Processes but the CCP is still associated, retain the existing CCP





In the same Edit Recipe Steps mode, provide an ‘Add CCP’ button/action on the same level as the ‘Add Step’ button
 




USDA Example:
HACCP Flow:

================ NXT-36742 ================
NXT-36742 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Recipe Rework - Recipe Steps - HACCP - Add/Edit CCP
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: Not Required
Epic: NXT-26823 — IM - Item Type Consolidation - Recipe Rework  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Allow CCPs to be added to the recipe like steps

Allow added CCPs to be edited for Critical Limit and Corrective Action

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a Nutritionist I want the HACCP options available on recipes so I can easily assign required control points for my recipe preparation.
Requirements
Once HACCP Process is selected, provide the ‘Add Control Point’ sub-option to the Add Steps button
 

This would display an add/edit view very similar to Add/Edit Step but would provide other fields in place of Directions
Split button example:  



e.g. if user selected ‘No Cook’ process, they will only see CCPs associated with No Cook (per the config page)
If user selects No HACCP Process, disable the Add Control Point button/option





First, users must select one of the CCPs from the list of CCPs configured for their selected process
Use the ‘Ingredient Search’ card as a ‘Control Point selection’ card

Show the list of associated Control Points in the side card

Show the Description and Critical Limit

Show the Corrective Action in an accordion expandable area

Allow a single CCP to be selected, at which point it can be added to the Steps



Upon selection, the remaining fields can be populated (in the Steps card area)
Critical Limit 
the temperature field(s) should be edited, but exactly how depends on which CCP is selected 



Corrective Action
this will load with the configured value but can be edited





With the required fields populated (via default or user values), the CCP can be saved

Insert CCPs into the recipe as steps  


USDA Example:
HACCP Flow:

================ NXT-30155 ================
NXT-30155 | [Done Done] [Story] [P:High] IM - Items - Create New Item - POS Info
Module: Item Management  |  components: Item Management  |  RN Required: -
Epic: NXT-30027 — IM - Item Details Overhaul  (call read_epic for full epic context + sibling tickets)

## Acceptance Criteria  [TIER 1 — agreed workflow]
Enable the POS Info tab if the user specifies the item as a POS Item

Load the POS attributes and Pricing section under the POS tab

Maintain existing logic, rules, etc.

Separate pricing for Students and Adults with tabs

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a District Admin I want a POS Info tab so I can enable it to be sold on the POS, configure POS attributes and set the pricing. 
Requirements
Enable and load the POS Info tab if the user specifies the item as a POS Item during creation, per  
color the tab as red to indicate missing mandatory attributes

Incomplete: flagged as POS but no mandatory attributes

Partial: mandatory attributes but no pricing

Complete: mandatory attributes and pricing for at least some groups



Display the main attributes in the header section
Button Name (English) - Mandatory, freetext

Button Name (Spanish) - freetext

Meal Type - dropdown
Optional, but required if using Generic Item Pricing



POS Item Category - dropdown

Flags (checkboxes)
Meal

Vendable

Generic Item





Display the Pricing section
Provide the flag attributes
Taxable

DM Reimbursable Meal

Use Generic Item Pricing
this shows another dropdown if the user checks this

Generic Items - dropdown, Mandatory

This requires a Meal Type to be selected





Display the Pricing grid
Separate Students and Adults with a tab

Provide the ‘Set All Prices’ option which can be used on one tab at a time (in Edit only)

for both view and edit mode

Remove the ‘DM Reimbursable Meal’ option from POS Only Items
hide the option in view/edit mode

do not delete/remove code; just set flag to False

(empty)

Provide the identifying information fields (Description + global info)
Require Description



Provide Tags field

Provide a Save button for continuing

## Release Notes  [TIER 1 — customer-facing voice]
(empty)

## Description  [TIER 3 — lowest trust]
As a District Admin I want the ability to create a new item and provide the identifying information.
Notes:
Use a new entry point; we will replace the existing button once complete

Only focusing on List View for now

Use new APIs / Components where possible
to ensure fresh approach 




Figma link
Requirements
Load the new initial item creation screen upon click on +New Item button

Show a toggle/switch/checkbox for the Active flag 
defaulted to Active



Display the identifying information fields
Item Description (formerly Name) - Mandatory
allow up to 250 characters to be typed (though the text field does not need to show all 250 characters at once)



Brand Name (dropdown)
Note: Brand Name will no longer be dependent on Manufacturer selection

keep the ‘Manage Brands’ shortcut 
future permission based





Product Code

Manufacturer (dropdown)
keep the ‘Manage Manufacturer’ shortcut 
future permission based







Display Data Source dropdown
only Local for now

we may choose to hide this altogether until we are providing proper Data Source support



Provide the Tags field component

Provide the check for duplicate items
as currently exists



Provide Save & Continue button which would advance to the next step and load more of the screen
Upon first Save, the item is officially created and assigned an Item #
*Note: at some point we will allow users to type their own Item # and it would be a user-defined field



Load next section after Save

Configuration - Page
Item Categories - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil, Delete for deactivate icon, Add for +New button



Common Measures - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil for local measures, Delete for trash icon, Add for +New button



Custom Allergens - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil, Delete for deactivate icon, Add for +New button



Menu Item Categories - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil, Delete for deactivate icon, Add for +New button



Storage Categories - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil, Delete for deactivate icon, Add for +New button



Valuation Groups - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil, Delete for deactivate icon, Add for +New button



HACCP Configuration - Sub-Page
Edit for pencil for locally created processes or CCPs, Delete for trash icon, Add for both +Add buttons

(empty)
